chapters.title: The Lost Mittens
chapters.text:
Three little kittens lived with their mother cat. Their names were Mittsy, Bitsy, and Ditsy. Each kitten had warm red mittens. One cold morning, they wanted to go outside to play in the snow.

They put on their mittens and ran outside. They played and played in the snow. They built snowballs and snow cats. When it was time for lunch, something terrible happened.

"Oh no!" cried Mittsy. "Where are my mittens?" "Mine are gone too!" said Bitsy. "And mine!" wailed Ditsy. The three little kittens had lost their mittens! They walked slowly back to the house.

Mother Cat was waiting with hot soup. But when she saw them without mittens, she was not happy. "You lost your mittens? You naughty kittens! No soup until you find them!"

comprehension.question: What are the names of the three kittens?
Why was Mother Cat upset with the kittens?

informationalText.title: Honeybees
informationalText.content:
Honeybees are amazing insects that live together in groups called colonies. A colony can have thousands of bees working together! The bees build their homes out of wax in places like hollow trees or special boxes called hives. Each bee has a job to do, like gathering food, taking care of baby bees, or protecting the hive.
Honeybees make honey by collecting nectar from flowers. They use their long tongues to suck up the sweet liquid from inside the flowers. Back at the hive, they turn the nectar into honey and store it in waxy cells. People have been keeping bees for thousands of years because honey tastes good and never spoils!

questions.question: What is a group of bees called?
How do bees make honey?
options: by collecting nectar from flowers and turning it into honey
by eating sugar and making it into honey
by finding honey in the forest

chapters.title: Searching High and Low
chapters.text:
The three little kittens were very sad. Their mother would not give them any food until they found their lost mittens. Their tummies were starting to growl.

"We must find our mittens," said Mittsy. "Let's look everywhere!" The kittens started searching inside the house. First, they looked under their beds. No mittens there.

Next, they searched in the toy box. They found balls and blocks, but no mittens. They looked behind the big chair where Father Cat liked to nap. Still no mittens.

The kittens looked in every room of the house. They searched high and low. They looked in corners and under rugs. But their mittens were nowhere to be found.

vocabulary.definition: to make a low, rumbling sound (like a hungry stomach)

comprehension.question: Why were the kittens' tummies growling?
Where did the kittens look first for their mittens?

informationalText.title: Apple Trees
informationalText.content:
Apple trees are fruit trees that grow in many parts of the world. These trees can live for a very long time, sometimes over 100 years! Apple trees need good soil, sunshine, and water to grow healthy and strong. In the spring, apple trees bloom with beautiful white or pink flowers that smell sweet.
After the flowers fall off, tiny apples start to grow. It takes all summer for the apples to get big and ripe. Different kinds of apple trees make different types of apples. Some apples are red, some are green, and some are yellow. People pick apples in the fall when they are ready to eat.

questions.question: When do apple trees bloom with flowers?
What do apple trees need to grow?

chapters.title: Back Outside
chapters.text:
The three little kittens put on their winter coats and went back outside. The snow was still falling softly. "Let's think," said Mittsy. "Where did we play first?"

"We built a snowman by the big tree," remembered Bitsy. The kittens hurried over to the tree. They looked all around the snowman they had made.

They dug in the snow with their paws. "Nothing here," sighed Ditsy. Then Mittsy had an idea. "Remember when we were throwing snowballs at the garden gate?"

The kittens hurried to the garden gate. They started digging and digging. Suddenly, Bitsy felt something soft and warm in the snow. They had found all their mittens!

comprehension.question: Where did the kittens find their mittens?

informationalText.title: Fire Trucks
informationalText.content:
Fire trucks are special vehicles that help firefighters save people and put out fires. These big red trucks carry lots of important equipment like hoses, ladders, and axes. Fire trucks also have a tank that holds hundreds of gallons of water. When there is a fire, the truck can pump water through long hoses to spray on the flames.
Fire trucks have loud sirens and flashing lights to warn other drivers to get out of the way. The firefighters need to get to fires quickly to help people and stop the fire from spreading. Some fire trucks have very tall ladders that can reach high windows in tall buildings. This helps firefighters rescue people who are trapped upstairs.

questions.question: What equipment do fire trucks carry?
Why do fire trucks have sirens and flashing lights?
options: to warn other drivers to get out of the way

chapters.title: Good News for Mother
chapters.text:
The three little kittens were so excited! They had found their lost mittens. Now Mother Cat would not be angry anymore. And maybe she would give them some food!

They ran as fast as they could back to the house. "Mother! Mother!" they called as they burst through the door. Mother Cat looked up from her knitting.

The kittens held up their mittens proudly. "Look! We found our mittens!" said Mittsy. "All of them!" added Bitsy. "They were by the garden gate!" explained Ditsy.

Mother Cat smiled for the first time that day. "What! You found your mittens? You good little kittens! Now you shall have some pie!" The kittens cheered with joy.

vocabulary.definition: with pride; showing you feel good about something

comprehension.question: How did Mother Cat feel when the kittens found their mittens?
What did Mother Cat promise to give the kittens?

informationalText.title: Penguins
informationalText.content:
Penguins are black and white birds that cannot fly, but they are amazing swimmers! Most penguins live in cold places near the ocean, especially around Antarctica. They have thick feathers and fat under their skin to keep them warm in the icy water. Penguins waddle when they walk on land, but they can slide on their bellies across the ice.
Penguins eat fish, squid, and tiny sea animals called krill. They can dive deep underwater and hold their breath for a long time while they hunt for food. Baby penguins are called chicks, and their parents take turns keeping the eggs warm and bringing food. Penguin families stay together and help take care of each other.

questions.question: Where do most penguins live?
options: in cold places near the ocean

chapters.title: A Messy Mistake
chapters.text:
The three little kittens hung their wet mittens on hooks by the warm fire. Steam rose from the wool as they began to dry. Then the kittens sat down to eat their pie.

It was the best apple pie they had ever tasted! The pie was so good that the kittens ate very quickly. They were so hungry from searching for their mittens all day.

As they ate, pie filling dripped on their paws. Apple pieces fell on their fur. Soon the kittens were quite messy. When they put on their mittens, something terrible happened again.

Mother Cat looked at them and frowned. "What! You soiled your mittens? You naughty kittens!" The mittens were covered with pie filling and crumbs. "Oh no!" cried the kittens.

comprehension.question: How did the kittens' mittens get dirty?
What was the pie made of?

informationalText.title: The Sun
informationalText.content:
The Sun is a star that gives Earth light and heat. Even though the Sun looks small in the sky, it is actually huge – much, much bigger than Earth! The Sun is so hot that nothing can live on it. The temperature on the Sun is thousands of degrees, hot enough to melt any metal instantly.
Without the Sun, there would be no life on Earth. Plants need sunlight to grow, and animals need plants for food. The Sun also makes the water cycle work by heating up water and turning it into clouds. During the day, the Sun lights up our part of Earth, and at night, our part of Earth faces away from the Sun so it gets dark.

questions.question: Why do we need the Sun?
options: it gives Earth light and heat that plants and animals need
it makes pretty colors in the sky

chapters.title: Clean Mittens, Happy Hearts
chapters.text:
Mother Cat shook her head at her messy little kittens. "You must wash your mittens before you can have any milk for dinner," she said. The kittens looked at their dirty mittens.

Mother Cat showed them how to fill a basin with warm, soapy water. The kittens took turns washing their mittens. They scrubbed gently to get out all the pie filling.

They rinsed them in clean water. "Look how clean they are now!" said Bitsy. The mittens were bright red again. "You washed your mittens? You good little kittens! Now you shall have some milk!"

From that day on, the three little kittens never lost their mittens again. And they always ate their pie very, very carefully. They had learned important lessons about taking care of their things.

vocabulary.definition: a bowl-shaped container for holding water

comprehension.question: What did the kittens use to wash their mittens?
What lesson did the kittens learn?
options:
Never eat pie
 
Answer Key
Chapter 1

informationalText.title: Librarians
informationalText.content:
Librarians are people who work in libraries to help others find books and information. They know where to find all kinds of books – stories, science books, history books, and more. Librarians also help people use computers to research topics they want to learn about. Many librarians have special training to learn how to organize books and help people.
Besides helping people find books, librarians do many other jobs. They read stories to children, plan fun programs and activities, and teach people how to use the library. Some libraries have librarians who specialize in helping children or teenagers. Librarians work hard to make sure everyone feels welcome in the library and can find what they need.

questions.question: What is the main job of librarians?
options: to help people find books and information

questions.question: What other activities do librarians do?
